criteriaResults.summary: Runtime gate is manifest-first and package-local, but query-ready lineage still depends on catalog/books.yaml plus artifacts/checkpoints projection to derive final graph capability.
residualRisks: Manifest validation can succeed while capability derivation still fails if catalog projection state cannot be rebuilt.

criteriaResults.summary: Required GraphRAG artifact set, file checksums, bytes, and required flags are defined and enforced fail-closed.
residualRisks: Correctness still depends on the producer continuing to emit the expected closure.

criteriaResults.summary: Candidate validation, publish marker ordering, live validation, and marker removal exist, but publish is not a staging-directory atomic rename into liveRoot.
residualRisks: A live-root write window still exists and is hidden by marker semantics instead of directory generation isolation.

criteriaResults.summary: Required artifact metadata rows and producer run summaries cover producer run, step, input fingerprint, tool/schema version, createdAt, and upstream hashes, and incomplete lineage fails closed.
residualRisks: Semantic quality of upstreamArtifactHashes still relies on producer-side metadata generation.

criteriaResults.summary: Producer run evidence is semantically bound to artifact rows through run.artifactIds or durable refresh bindings, with stage and provider fingerprint checks.
residualRisks: New shared artifact refresh patterns would need matching durable refresh binding logic.

criteriaResults.summary: Runtime compatibility now fail-closes on package schema, layout, qmd schema, GraphRAG artifact schema, min runtime version, provider fingerprint, embedding dimension, and output/parquet/LanceDB/artifact metadata digests.
residualRisks: Embedding model identity is not checked as a distinct explicit field, but the implemented contract checks the runtime compatibility inputs and digests that are currently normative.

criteriaResults.summary: Capability loading and projection filtering remain book/document/source scoped and skip unrelated damaged run records.
residualRisks: Projection remains a cache layer, so isolation correctness still assumes projection rebuild continues to respect the same scope filters.

criteriaResults.summary: Runtime gate and package validation reject provider payload roots, logs, debug, lock files, and other forbidden sensitive material.
residualRisks: Future sensitive path conventions will require denylist maintenance.

criteriaResults.summary: Stable diagnostics and publish marker rollback are implemented, but there is no explicit prior-generation liveRoot preservation or interrupted publish recovery based on generation swap.
residualRisks: A failed live publish can be hidden by removing the marker, but the contract-grade last-good generation rollback story remains incomplete.

criteriaResults.summary: Current tests cover runtime digest mismatch, layout/embedding mismatch, missing metadata rows, forged lineage, provider payload exclusion, stale catalog rebuild, missing producer runs, readonly package lock non-creation, and backfill conflicts.
residualRisks: Staged import rollback is still not exercised as a directory-generation test.

findings.summary: Manifest-first runtime gate exists, but direct query capability derivation still requires catalog/books.yaml plus book-state projections.
Publish ordering is hardened, but atomic directory replacement is still absent.
Publish marker rollback exists, but previous live generation rollback is not implemented as an explicit recoverable package lifecycle.
R8 partial is closed for the current contract surface; remaining ambiguity is limited to whether embedding model identity should become an explicit checked field in a future contract revision.
Producer lineage checks now cover the requested completeness dimensions for query-ready artifacts and fail closed when producer evidence is missing or forged.
Readonly package .lock creation concern is closed: runtime validation performs read-only package access and tests assert no .lock files are written.

commandsConsidered.reason: User supplied current mainline validation status already includes build success; this audit role performed focused read-only verification plus targeted test execution.
